chunks.text: Sanjam Garg is an Associate Professor at UC Berkeley. His research interests are in cryptography and security. He obtained his Ph.D. from UCLA in 2013 and his undergraduate degree from IIT Delhi in 2008. Prof. Garg is the recipient of various honors such as the ACM Doctoral Dissertation Award, the Sloan Research Fellowship and the IIT Delhi Graduates of the Last Decade Award. Prof. Garg's research has been recognized with best paper awards at EUROCRYPT 2013, CRYPTO 2017 and EUROCRYPT 2018.
chunks.question: What are Sanjam Garg's research interests?

chunks.text: Education: 2013, Ph.D., Computer Science, University of California, Los Angeles. 2008, B.Tech, Computer Science and Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology, Delhi. Research Areas: Theory (THY), Security (SEC). Research Centers: Berkeley Center for Responsible, Decentralized Intelligence (RDI), Simons Institute for the Theory of Computing (SITC). Teaching Schedule: CS 276. Cryptography, TuTh 11:00-12:29, Soda 310.
chunks.question: Where did Sanjam Garg earn his Ph.D.?

chunks.text: Selected Honors and Awards: Bakar Fellows Spark Award (2023), Foundations of Computer Science (FOCS) Test of Time Award (2023), Sloan Research Fellow (2020), Okawa Research Grant (2016), IACR EUROCRYPT Best Paper Award (2013), ACM Doctoral Dissertation Award (2013).
chunks.question: Which award did Sanjam Garg receive in 2020?
